text: The surgeon demonstrates the vessel-first medial-to-lateral approach for a laparoscopic right hemicolectomy. The ileocolic pedicle is lifted, and an incision is made below it to develop a plane. The artery and vein are identified, ligated, and divided. The mesentery is lifted, and medial-to-lateral mobilization is performed using blunt and sharp dissection until reaching the bowel laterally and superiorly. The duodenum is separated from the mesentery, and lateral attachments are taken down to complete the dissection.
